may_claim: W trace closes as on-shell EW codomain completion under separate PDG2025 and CMS2026 ledgers; max |z|=0.528997.
must_not_claim: physical-final global EW fit, zero residual, or unauthorized PDG+CMS combined average.
open_boundary: full EW global-fit/correlation ledger if one wants global-combination finality

declared_codomain: pole masses with APF envelope; QED running boundary typed
may_claim: charged-lepton APF pole branch is complete within APF/CODATA envelope; QED route and external ledger boundary are typed.
must_not_claim: QED-running final, physical-final lepton masses, zero residual, or QED residual fitting.
open_boundary: full external QED running coefficient/threshold/covariance ledger if running masses are requested

may_claim: charm APF trace exports to MSbar self-scale candidate and is inside PDG2025 quoted 90CL envelope.
must_not_claim: physical-final charm mass, zero residual, pole mass, or full perturbative/lattice QCD finality.
open_boundary: full QCD covariance/evaluator finality beyond PDG comparison envelope

may_claim: bottom APF trace exports to MSbar self-scale candidate and is inside PDG2025 quoted 90CL envelope.
must_not_claim: physical-final bottom mass, zero residual, pole mass, or full perturbative/lattice QCD finality.
open_boundary: full QCD covariance/evaluator finality beyond PDG comparison envelope

may_claim: top closes in the finite-resolution direct-reconstruction MSR codomain; pole branch is explicitly blocked by four-loop conversion.
must_not_claim: top pole final, MC equality, physical-final top mass, zero residual, or target-fitted reconstruction scale.
open_boundary: external MC calibration ledger if one wants a MC-parameter interpretation; pole branch remains knocked out

may_claim: APF light-quark source side is complete and identity/scalar transport is knocked out.
must_not_claim: light-quark numerical export to MSbar(2 GeV), physical-final light masses, or identity transport.
open_boundary: source-independent chiral/lattice evaluator U_chilat with scale setting, action, Z_m, EM/isospin, matching, covariance
